signals.title: AUD/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + Data Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7053. USD is the strongest G10 currency this week, averaging +0.687% against its peers, while AUD is the weakest at -0.657%. This 1.344% spread highlights significant bilateral divergence. The AUD CESI at -8 (moderate negative bias) confirms data disappointment, while USD CESI at +29 (strong positive) supports USD strength. This fundamental divergence is driving AUD/USD lower. Watch for RBA's next meeting on 16 Jun for any shift in policy stance.

signals.title: USD/JPY — BoJ Intervention Risk + Carry
signals.text: USD/JPY at 160.17, trading above the 160.00 level that has previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings. The 289bp Fed-BoJ OIS carry premium (SOFR 3.62% vs TONA 0.73%) continues to sustain the bid, despite JPY CESI at +16 (strong positive). JPY COT LF net short -105,136 (29.5% long) indicates a crowded carry trade. Intervention risk is elevated on a sustained break above 160.00. The next BoJ meeting (mkt: 50% hike priced) on 16 Jun is a key catalyst.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs Retail Divergence + Volatility
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5816. COT LF net short -27,797 (15.1% long) indicates extreme speculative short positioning. Retail sentiment is extremely long at 87% (Myfxbook), creating a significant institutional vs. retail divergence. The options market shows NZD/USD IV 26.0% vs HV30 8.7%, with a VRP of +17.3%, signaling elevated hedging demand. This combination suggests potential for a sharp short-squeeze if price extends higher, catching retail long and institutional short offside. Watch for RBNZ's next meeting on 8 Jul for any policy shift.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Retail Extreme + Carry + BoC Outlook
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.3956. Retail sentiment is extremely short at 86% (Myfxbook), indicating a crowded contrarian position against the prevailing trend. The 138bp Fed-BoC OIS carry premium (SOFR 3.62% vs CORRA 2.24%) structurally favors USD. Recent headlines from NBC suggest the Bank of Canada is urged to focus on unemployment, implying a dovish bias that is likely to limit CAD upside. This retail extreme against strong carry and a potentially dovish BoC creates significant short-squeeze risk for CAD. The next BoC meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 10 Jun is a key event.
